subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the social structure of the Old Regime in France
Identify the economic and political causes of the revolution
Trace the timeline of major events like the Fall of the Bastille
Understand the significance of the Declaration of the Rights of Man
Learn about the impact of the revolution on women and the abolition of slavery
Analyze the legacy of the revolution for modern democratic societies

lesson_plan.title: French Society During the Late 18th Century
topics_covered: The Estate System: Clergy, Nobility, and Third Estate
Feudal privileges and the tax burden
Economic crisis and the struggle to survive (Subsistence Crisis)
teaching_strategy: Visual aid: Pyramid diagram of the Three Estates
Storytelling: A day in the life of a peasant vs. a noble
student_activities: Draw the social pyramid of the Three Estates
List the taxes paid by the Third Estate (Tithes and Taille)
assessment: Oral quiz on who belonged to which estate

lesson_plan.title: Outbreak of the Revolution
topics_covered: Meeting of the Estates General (1789)
Formation of the National Assembly and the Tennis Court Oath
The Fall of the Bastille (14th July 1789)
teaching_strategy: Discussion on why the Bastille was hated by the people
student_activities: Create a small timeline of events leading to 14th July
Role-play: The meeting at the Tennis Court
assessment: Questioning on the significance of 14th July

lesson_plan.title: France Becomes a Constitutional Monarchy
topics_covered: The Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen
Separation of Powers (Legislature, Executive, Judiciary)
Difference between Active and Passive citizens
teaching_strategy: Flowchart analysis of the 1791 Political System
Reading key rights from the textbook
student_activities: Identify modern rights that started here (Freedom of speech, etc.)

lesson_plan.title: The Abolition of Monarchy & The Reign of Terror
topics_covered: France becomes a Republic
The role of Jacobins and Maximilian Robespierre
The Reign of Terror and the Guillotine
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the shift from Monarchy to Republic
student_activities: Write a short paragraph on the Jacobin Club
Map work: Identify Paris and the spread of 'The Great Fear'
assessment: MCQ on the leader of the Jacobins

lesson_plan.title: Did Women Have a Revolution?
topics_covered: Life of women in 18th century France
Women’s political clubs (The Society of Revolutionary and Republican Women)
Olympe de Gouges and the fight for equal rights
teaching_strategy: Comparative discussion: Rights of men vs. rights of women
Biographical sketch of Olympe de Gouges
student_activities: List the demands made by French women
Discussion: When did women finally get the right to vote in France?
assessment: Short answer test on women's clubs

lesson_plan.title: The Abolition of Slavery & The Rise of Napoleon
topics_covered: The Triangular Slave Trade
Abolition of slavery in French colonies (1794/1848)
Napoleon Bonaparte as a 'modernizer' and conqueror
teaching_strategy: Map pointing: The Triangular Trade (Europe, Africa, Americas)
student_activities: Draw the triangular trade route on a world map
Brainstorm: Why was Napoleon seen as a liberator initially?
assessment: Paragraph writing on the abolition of slavery

lesson_plan.title: Revolution in Everyday Life & Legacy
topics_covered: Abolition of censorship
Freedom of press and its impact
Global legacy: Influence on India (Tipu Sultan, Raja Rammohan Roy)
teaching_strategy: Classroom debate: Impact of a free press
Discussion on how the revolution changed daily habits/dress
student_activities: Create a poster showing revolutionary symbols (The broken chain, Red cap, etc.)
Research note: Tipu Sultan and the French Revolution

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Evaluation
topics_covered: Recap of all major sections
Key dates and vocabulary review
Final doubt clearing
student_activities: Complete a comprehensive mind map of the revolution

teaching_aids: NCERT History Textbook (India and the Contemporary World - I)
evaluation_methods: Accuracy of diagrams (Social Pyramid, Political System)
teacher_tip: Use the revolutionary symbols (like the Broken Chain or the Sceptre) as visual cues to help students remember abstract concepts like 'Freedom' or 'Royal Power'. Relate the 'Declaration of Rights' to the Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution.
